utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi virginia how are you today what brings you in

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing alright i started seeing this new pcp last year and you know she has been doing a lot of changes to my medication and making sure everything is up to date and she my noticed that my blood pressure has been quite high so she added to medications but and but i you know i've been taking them i've been really good and i i before i was n't but now i am and we're still having a hard time controlling my blood pressure so she thought it would be a good idea for me to see you especially since she noted some on my last blood work she said something about my kidneys

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay yeah so okay let's before i dive into a lot of that tell me a little bit about how you've been feeling

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i would say you know most of the days i feel fine i'm still busy at work i definitely can tell though when my blood pressure is high

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay you measure it at home you you you measure your blood pressure at home

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i she wanted me to get a blood pressure cuff so i did start getting checking my blood pressures probably like a few times a week

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and so then i noticed that it has been getting higher the other day was even as high as one seventy over ninety

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: wow

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i did call my pcp and she increased the meds again

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah okay now i i just have a couple questions about that are you using a an electronic blood pressure recorder or do you have somebody help you at home

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah she i have a a electronic one an electronic arm one

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay yeah that's good that's good and have you ever tried do you go to cvs at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i i do but i've noticed like since the pandemic i do n't see the blood pressures anymore

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay yeah i i thought the one down on main street they i thought they just brought that one back so

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: did they

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's good to know

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you may wan na check that but okay so that's good but i what i'd like you to do with that is i'd like you to keep a record of them for me for my next visit with you so let's talk a little bit about your diet tell me how how is your diet what what are the what kind of foods do you like what do you eat normally

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alright do you want the honest answer

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well yeah that would be better

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i really you know with everything going on i really been trying to get better but i mean during football season it's really difficult i really love watching my games so have a lot of pizza wings subs like i said i've been trying to cut down especially on days where there is no games but it probably could be better

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay i think we all can say that but i do wan na just hey i do n't know that if you've tried it or not but there is a new restaurant down on fifth street and it is nothing but solids and i you know when i heard this i was like okay yeah it's just another these solids are absolutely amazing so if you ever get a chance yeah if you ever get a chance try try that i mean i think you would enjoy them because they're salads that they make are just out unbelievable so let me go ahead and i just have a few more questions and i'm gon na just ask these in in order and you just tell me and then we will come back and talk about them do you have any headaches

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: really just when my blood pressure gets really high i have some mild headaches but otherwise i do n't have it on a regular basis

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay what about chest pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no chest pain

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: shortness of breath

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no shortness of breath

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: even with exertion

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: even with exertion

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay do you have any swelling in your lower extremities at all that you noticed

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not if i'm on my feet for a long time i'll notice a little bit of swelling but otherwise no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and then a couple other family history questions anybody in the family have kidney disease or significant high blood pressure

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: both my parents do have high blood pressure and one of them did have kidney disease

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay and in the the the form that you filled out when you came in it says that you are on ten milligrams of norvasc daily and carvedilol twenty five milligrams twice a day is those the medicines you're on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes i was also on lisinopril before but with the adjustments yeah those are the ones i'm on

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and so here's where i think we are going to go do you take any nonsteroidals like advil or motrin or aleve

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah just once in a while for my like any knee pain or back pain that i have but again not like everyday

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and then lastly what kind of alcohol intake do you have you know do you consider how many drinks a week is really what i'm looking for

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'll have a couple of beers during the week and like one or two on the weekends

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay so lem me do a quick physical examination so i looked at your vitals when you came in today and your blood pressure it's still high it's one sixty nine over seventy four your heart rate was eighty eight and your oxygenation was ninety eight percent so those are all fairly good except that blood pressure's a little higher than we'd like to see now when i look at your neck i do n't see any jugular vein distention and i'm gon na listen here real quick no i do n't hear any carotid bruits i'm gon na listen to your lungs okay your lungs are clear and let me listen quickly to your heart i do hear that a two over six systolic ejection murmur and we'll we're gon na have to take a little bit look extra look at that that's when i i can hear an extra sound when i'm listening to your heart and you do have a small amount of one plus pitting edema bilaterally now i did so you do have that your diagnosis is uncontrolled hypertension you know and i think you're aware that that's what your your physician's been treating you for and most of the time this cause is is the cause of this is multifactorial it's not that there is just one thing causing it so we may need to be changing your medicine around and i'm gon na talk to your doctor but first thing before we make any more medication changes i want to order some tests first to rule out if there is any specific cause for this so first order will be a renal artery ultrasound and what i'm looking for there is that there is no areas of areas of narrowing in the the blood vessels of your kidneys that would be the cause of your hypertension in addition to that i'm gon na order a you get another urine collection some morning aldosterone levels reining levels and a twenty four hour urine and these things can really show me if there is any problems with your adrenal glands again this is a lot of big words but you know i'm i i'll write this all out for you i want you to decrease your alcohol i know you like those beers but let's bring it down to maybe one a week or two a week just to get those down lower and then your salt intake you need to be very judicious about decreasing that salt intake i'm gon na give you a referral to a nutritionist to discuss those changes for that you need and and they will help you get that cleared up and then finally stop taking any nonsteroidal medicines such as your advil or motrin the only thing i really want to want you taking is tylenol for any pain right now i am gon na prescribe one medicine and that's cardura four milligrams and i want you to take that once a day and that's good to see if that can help us with your blood pressure and then finally three weeks i'd like you to return i want you to record all of your blood pressures that you take over the next three weeks and bring them into the office but most importantly if you can try to take them at the same time everyday that would be beneficial for me any questions for me

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i i just it's a lot so i i'm hoping this will work and this will get it under control

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i i think you know this will be you know this we're gon na spend some time together so i'm glad to have you as a patient but you know we got ta try to get this under control and i'm gon na i'll be talking to your pcp just to let them know that you know what my plans are and we'll stay real in sync on treating this as we move forward does that sound like a plan

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds good thank you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay take care i'll talk to you later

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay alright bye

detail_list.summary: The patient presents for evaluation of high blood pressure.
Hypertension
The patient reports difficulty controlling blood pressure despite medication adjustments and mentions concerns regarding kidney function.
The doctor asks if the patient monitors her blood pressure at home.
The patient monitors her blood pressure a few times a week using an electronic cuff.
The patient reports a recent blood pressure reading of 170/90 mmHg.
The doctor instructs the patient to keep a log of home blood pressure readings for the next visit.
The doctor asks about the patient's dietary habits.
The patient reports a diet high in salt, including pizza, wings, and subs.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences headaches.
The patient reports mild headaches when her blood pressure is elevated.
The doctor asks if the patient has chest pain.
The patient denies chest pain.
The doctor asks if the patient has shortness of breath.
The patient denies shortness of breath.
The doctor asks if the patient has shortness of breath with exertion.
The patient denies shortness of breath with exertion.
The doctor asks if the patient has lower extremity swelling.
The patient reports occasional lower extremity swelling after being on her feet for a long time.
The doctor asks about family history of kidney disease or hypertension.
The patient reports both parents have hypertension and one has kidney disease.
The patient confirms taking Norvasc 10 mg daily and carvedilol 25 mg twice daily.
The doctor asks if the patient takes nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs.
The patient reports occasional use of anti-inflammatories for pain.
The doctor asks about the patient's alcohol intake.
The patient reports drinking a couple of beers during the week and 1 to 2 on the weekend.
Physical exam findings include BP 169/74, HR 88, SpO2 98%, grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, and 1+ pitting edema in bilateral lower extremities; no jugular vein distension or carotid bruits noted.

The doctor orders a renal artery ultrasound, urine collection, morning aldosterone, renin levels, and 24-hour urine; prescribes Cardura 4 mg daily; advises reducing alcohol and salt intake; instructs to stop anti-inflammatories and use Tylenol; and schedules a follow-up in 3 weeks with a blood pressure log.
